commands.repair_reason: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the PR already contains the narrow code/test changes and should proceed through normal maintainer and CI review.; Cleared: The diff only changes typed session metadata, agent-facing tool/prompt text, tests, and snapshots; it does not touch secrets, dependencies, CI, install, or package execution surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional `resumable` boolean to Gateway and agent-facing session rows, forwards it through `sessions_list`, and updates agent tool/prompt text plus focused tests and snapshots.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +5. Total +25 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: no. live current-main reproduction was run in this read-only review, but the source path is high-confidence: current main exposes terminal-looking statuses without `resumable`, while `sessions_send` can reactivate ended subagent runs. The PR screenshots provide after-fix real behavior proof for the same-session follow-up path.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the PR already contains the narrow code/test changes and should proceed through normal maintainer and CI review.

target.title: fix: add resumable field to prevent duplicate session spawns
target.body: ## Problem Session `status` field values (`done`, `failed`, `timeout`, `killed`) mislead agents into treating sessions as permanently terminated, causing duplicate `sessions_spawn` calls instead of resuming existing sessions via `sessions_send`. Closes #64103 ## Root Cause The `status` field reflects **last-turn communication state**, not session lifecycle state. However, nothing in the data model or tool descriptions makes this distinction explicit — so LLM agents interpret `failed`/`timeout`/`done` as terminal and spawn new sessions. `reactivateCompletedSubagentSession()` in `session-subagent-reactivation.ts` confirms that sessions in these states **are** resumable via `sessions_send`. ## Solution (Incremental, backward-compatible) Following [ClawSweeper's review](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/64103#issuecomment-*) recommendation of additive metadata over breaking rename: 1. **Add `resumable?: boolean` field** to `GatewaySessionRow` and `SessionListRow` — non-running sessions set `resumable: true` 2. **Compute in `buildGatewaySessionRow()`** — `resolvedStatus !== "running"` → `resumable: true`; running sessions omit it 3. **Pass through in `sessions-list-tool`** — agent sees `resumable` in output 4. **Update tool description** — `describeSessionsListTool()` now explains status semantics 5. **Update system prompt** — `sessions_list` summary clarifies status is last-turn only, prefer `sessions_send` ## Changed Files | File | Change | |---|---| | `src/gateway/session-utils.types.ts` | Add `resumable?: boolean` to `GatewaySessionRow` | | `src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts` | Add `resumable?: boolean` to `SessionListRow` | | `src/gateway/session-utils.ts` | Compute `resolvedStatus` + `resolvedResumable` in `buildGatewaySessionRow()` | | `src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts` | Pass `resumable` through to agent output | | `src/agents/tool-description-presets.ts` | Add status semantics note to `describeSessionsListTool()` | | `src/agents/system-prompt.ts` | Add status guidance to `sessions_list` tool summary | | `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` | Add `resumable: undefined` to test expectation | | `src/gateway/session-utils.subagent.test.ts` | Add `resumable` assertions for done/failed/timeout/running states | ## Test Results ``` 12 test files passed, 194 tests passed oxfmt format check: all passed Prompt snapshots regenerated ``` ## Real behavior proof **behavior**: Session `status` values `done`/`failed`/`timeout` mislead agents into spawning duplicates instead of resuming via `sessions_send`. The `resumable` field makes recoverability explicit. **environment**: Windows 11, OpenClaw 2026.5.28 built from source on branch `fix/session-status-resumable-64103` **steps**: 1. Started OpenClaw locally (`pnpm dev`) 2. Triggered a conversation, let the session complete (`status: "done"`) 3. Called `sessions_list` — confirmed `status: "done (resumable)"` appears in output 4. Sent a follow-up message to the done session — agent resumed and replied successfully 5. Called `sessions_list` again — confirmed same session, tokens increased from 17,898 to 18,542, no duplicate spawned **evidence**: **observedResult**: `sessions_list` correctly surfaces `resumable` for non-running sessions. The same session is resumed with accumulating tokens (17,898 → 18,542); no duplicate session is spawned. **notTested**: N/A — the core resumable logic is fully covered by unit tests (`session-utils.subagent.test.ts` with assertions for all status values).
target.author: w1ndcn
